background: A South African study compared whether participating in a 5-year follow-up interview was associated with having medical aid (similar to health insurance).

task.query: Table 19.5A tabulates the results for all subjects combined. Calculate the prevalence of medical aid in the group that was followed up and the group that was lost to follow up. Describe the relationship between follow up and having medical aid.
task.answer: Among the 416 children who were followed-up, $46(11.1 \\%)$ had medical aid. In contrast, 195 of $1174(16.6 \\%)$ who were not followed-up had medical aid. Therefore, there is a negative association between follow-up and having medical aid.

task.query: Subjects were subclassified by race. Table 19.5B shows the results for white participants. Describe the relationship in this stratum.
task.answer: Among the white participants, 10 of $12(83 \\%)$ who were followed-up had medical aid. One-hundred and four of the 126 white subjects who were not followed-up $(83 \\%)$ had medical aid. Therefore, there is no association between follow-up and having medical aid in this stratum.

task.query: Data for black participants are shown in Table 19.5C. Analyze the results for this stratum.
task.answer: Nine percent of both groups had medical aid. Therefore, there is no association between follow-up and having medical aid in this stratum.

task.query: Discuss whether race confounded the crude analysis in part (a).
task.answer: Race is associated with follow-up and medical aid coverage. Therefore, race confounded the crude analysis in part (a).

meta_info.caption: Is participating in a follow-up survey associated with having medical aid?
